milestone: Builder Project cloud backup, Save button removed
slice: A deliberate, disclosed extension of Supabase's scope beyond the Official/Personal Theme Repositories: every in-progress Builder Project used to live only in this browser's localStorage, with no backup anywhere. New builder_projects Supabase table (owner-scoped via the same auth.uid() pattern the Personal Theme Repository uses) plus new js/services/projectSync.js push a background copy on every edit (2s debounce), reusing ThemeRepositoryClient's already-established client/anonymous session rather than a second sign-in. Local-primary, cloud backup: localStorage remains the only source of truth the Workspace reads from; Supabase is best-effort. A second, honest header badge reports the background copy's own state (unavailable/pending/synced/error) completely independently of the pre-existing local-save badge. The Save button was removed outright -- every edit already autosaved synchronously and the local badge already reported that truth immediately, so a button that just re-ran the identical write added nothing real. Disclosed caveat: the anonymous session identity is itself a localStorage token, so a cleared browser loses that too -- this narrows real risk rather than eliminating it.
